description: Correction streams, rover connections, queue health, signal quality, authentication and process stats.

spec.description: Delivered frames / published frames per mountpoint. Equals the number of rovers successfully receiving each correction.
spec.title: Avg Rovers Reached per Frame

spec.description: Dot colour: green = data fresh (< 10 s), yellow = slightly stale (< 60 s), red = stale / offline. Circle size is proportional to the configured maximum rover range (mask), giving a visual indication of each station's coverage area in the network.
spec.title: Base Stations

spec.description: Median and 95th-percentile time between consecutive frames from each base station. Spikes in p95 indicate dropped or delayed frames. Typical RTCM correction rates are 0.2–1 Hz (0.5–5 s intervals).
spec.title: Frame Interval (p50 / p95)

spec.description: Seconds since the last frame was received from each mountpoint. Values above 30 s indicate a stale or offline base station.
spec.title: Time Since Last Frame

spec.description: Median carrier-to-noise density (CNR) per constellation from RTCM MSM4–7 messages. Healthy open-sky signals: 35–50 dBHz. Values below 30 dBHz suggest poor reception. Anomalously uniform CNR across all satellites (all satellites at exactly the same level) is a potential spoofing indicator.
spec.title: Signal CNR by Constellation (median dBHz)

spec.description: Median number of satellites tracked per MSM frame, by constellation. Sudden drops may indicate jamming, antenna obstruction, or loss of a constellation. Spoofed signals often show an unrealistically high or fixed satellite count.
spec.title: Satellites Tracked by Constellation (median)

spec.description: Rate of each RTCM message type received. Useful for verifying that the expected correction message set (e.g. 1077 GPS MSM7, 1087 GLONASS MSM7, 1097 Galileo MSM7, 1005 antenna reference) is being streamed.
spec.title: RTCM Message Rate by Type

spec.description: Rate of RTCM frames that failed to parse (CRC error, truncated message, unknown format). A non-zero rate may indicate link corruption, a misconfigured base station, or an unsupported encoding.
spec.title: RTCM Parse Errors / sec

title: NTRIP
